{{Game |Guid=75ca8cfa-9f3a-49f9-9aa5-a82d19a235fb |Name=1666 Amsterdam |Image=https://giantbomb.com/api/media/file/media--file--header-06-06-2026.jpg |BackgroundImage=https://giantbomb.com/api/media/file/media--file--ss_83ece33dd8c0d8928faf4f3e8521ff7b1f0b681e-1920x1080-06-06-2026.jpg |Deck=A story-driven action adventure game that is focused on witchcraft and thwarting centuries-old demons. |ReleaseDateType=Full |Concepts=Concepts/Steam, Concepts/Epic Games Store, Concepts/Cats, Concepts/Witch, Concepts/Polygonal 3D, Concepts/Third Person Perspective, Concepts/Female Protagonists, Concepts/Demon |Developers=Companies/Panache Digital Games |Genres=Genres/Action Adventure |Platforms=Platforms/PC |Publishers=Companies/Panache Digital Games |Themes=Themes/Horror }}

{{Game |Guid=fd91b2b4-3edc-47d0-bb56-c693669c5a1c |Name=2 Fights in 2 Tight Spaces |Image=Asfasfaew.jpg |BackgroundImage=https://giantbomb.com/api/media/file/media--file--ss_1d0253b726aabe0c909dace6a50401ed394cd594-1920x1080-06-08-2026.jpg |Deck=Agent 11 returns in Ground Shatter's tactical deck builder. |ReleaseDateType=Full |ReleaseDate=2026-06-07 |Concepts=Concepts/Sequel, Concepts/Digital Distribution, Concepts/Pre Release Public Testing, Concepts/Announced and Released on the Same Day |Developers=Companies/Ground Shatter |Franchises=Franchises/In Tight Spaces |Genres=Genres/Action, Genres/Strategy |Platforms=Platforms/PC |Publishers=Companies/Ground Shatter }}

{{Game |Guid=5e178b0b-ee55-4db7-aab9-4229a2ec2259 |Name=38-0 |Aliases=38-0 Premier League Draft Game |Image=38-0-browser-game.png |Deck=A free browser-based Premier League draft game where you build an all-time XI and simulate a full 38-match season with a Monte Carlo engine. |ReleaseDateType=None |Genres=Genres/Football, Genres/Simulation, Genres/Soccer, Genres/Sports }} == Overview ==
38-0 is a free, browser-based Premier League draft game where players build an all-time XI from legendary players across every era of English top-flight football. The game uses a Monte Carlo simulation engine to project how the selected team would perform across a full 38-match season.
== Gameplay ==
The core loop works as follows:
1. '''Spin a wheel''' which lands on a real Premier League club and season (e.g., Arsenal 2003-04, Leicester 2015-16, or even mid-table sides like Watford 2018-19). 2. '''Draft one player''' from that squad. Repeat until all 11 positions are filled. 3. '''Pick a formation''' from multiple options (4-3-3, 4-4-2, 3-5-2, and more). 4. '''Run the simulator''' — 1,000 Monte Carlo seasons modeled with a Poisson goal distribution, weighing formation fit, position fit, and legend chemistry.
== Simulation Engine ==
The match engine models each game as a Poisson distribution, where expected goals are derived from squad ratings. Formation fit and position fit matter — a striker forced into goal will tank results. Club chemistry provides bonuses for players who shared a real club season.
Two modes are available:
* '''Classic''' — ratings visible * '''Expert''' — ratings hidden, testing pure football knowledge
== External Links ==
* [https://www.38-0.pro/ Official Website - Play 38-0]
[[Category:Sports Games]] [[Category:Browser Games]] [[Category:Simulation Games]]

revision.comment: Restore image alignment, sizes and captions from legacy source (3030-35091)
revision.text:
{{Game | Name=Ace Combat: Assault Horizon Legacy | Guid=3030-35091 | Aliases=Ace Combat 3D: Cross Rumble | Deck=Initially announced under the title of "Ace Combat 3D", Ace Combat: Assault Horizon Legacy (named "Ace Combat 3D: Cross Rumble" in Japan) is an all new Ace Combat made from the ground up for Nintendo 3DS. | ReleaseDate=2011-11-15 | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Aerial_Combat,Concepts/Circle_Pad_Pro_Support,Concepts/Physical_3DS_Games_With_Complete_Manuals,Concepts/Polygonal_3D,Concepts/Stereoscopic_3D | Developers=Companies/Bandai_Namco_Entertainment | Franchises=Franchises/Ace_Combat | Genres=Genres/Action,Genres/Flight_Simulator | Locations=Locations/Dubai,Locations/Persian_Gulf,Locations/Strangereal | Objects= | Platforms=Platforms/Nintendo_3DS | Publishers=Companies/Bandai_Namco_Entertainment | Games=Games/Pilotwings_Resort,Games/Star_Fox_64_3D | Themes=Themes/Alternate_Historical | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/8/87790/2213146-box_acahl.png }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2213146-box_acahl.png","path":"8\/87790\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_avatar,square_small,scale_small,square_tiny,square_medium,scale_avatar,square_mini,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_super,scale_tiny,screen_kubrick","caption":"3DS box art (cropped)"},"background":[]}' />
==Overview== Ace Combat: Assault Horizon Legacy (Also know as Ace Combat 3D: Cross Rumble in Japan) is an action/flight game developed and published by [[Companies/Bandai_Namco_Entertainment|Namco Bandai Games]] for the [[Platforms/Nintendo_3DS|Nintendo 3DS]] platform.
==Setting==
{{GBFigure|src=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/8/86760/1989043-ace_combat_3d_cross_rumble_nintendo_3ds_1318520558_017.jpg|full=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/8/86760/1989043-ace_combat_3d_cross_rumble_nintendo_3ds_1318520558_017.jpg|align=right|width=320}} Ace Combat: Assault Horizon Legacy takes place in the [[Franchises/Ace_Combat|Ace Combat]] universe (also known as the [[Locations/Strangereal|Strangereal]] universe) rather than a real world setting, and its story events are a retelling of [[Games/Ace_Combat_2|Ace Combat 2]]. However, despite all locations and names of countries being fictional, real world aircraft are used.
==Gameplay==
{{GBFigure|src=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/8/86760/1989052-ace_combat_3d_cross_rumble_nintendo_3ds_1318520558_026.jpg|full=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/8/86760/1989052-ace_combat_3d_cross_rumble_nintendo_3ds_1318520558_026.jpg|align=right|width=320}} Assault Horizon Legacy is a flight combat title where players are pitted against ground targets and enemy planes. The gameplay is not on-rails meaning the player is able to fly toward any direction at all times over expansive environments.
Like [[Games/Ace_Combat_Assault_Horizon|Ace Combat: Assault Horizon for PS3 and Xbox 360]], Assault Horizon Legacy receives gameplay additions, namely in the form of single-button combat maneuvers, which no Ace Combat title previously had. However, the implementation of these additions are different in Assault Horizon Legacy when compared to [[Games/Ace_Combat_Assault_Horizon|Ace Combat: Assault Horizon for PS3 and Xbox 360]]. When close enough to an enemy aircraft, in Assault Horizon Legacy, the player can press a button which causes their aircraft to immediately swoop behind the enemy on its own, after which the player's aircraft is temporarily locked behind the enemy, during which time it is easier to get a hit on the enemy aircraft. The enemy aircraft, if not shot down, breaks away after only a few seconds, returning the gameplay to normal.
The Japanese version of the game titled Ace Combat 3D: Cross Rumble released in January 2012 with [[Concepts/Circle_Pad_Pro_Support|Circle Pad Pro Support]].

{{Game |Guid=fa9283eb-3a2b-4617-9fc3-0cc6ccc1d662 |Name=Anemorie |Image=https://giantbomb.com/api/media/file/media--file--anemorie-06-17-2026.jpg |BackgroundImage=https://giantbomb.com/api/media/file/media--file--anemorieb-06-17-2026.jpg |Deck=A narrative adventure about a girl in the 1990s who has the ability to see memories in objects. |ReleaseDateType=None |Concepts=Concepts/Indie, Concepts/Digital Distribution, Concepts/Steam, Concepts/2D, Concepts/Female Protagonists, Concepts/Paranormal, Concepts/Visual Novel, Concepts/Cats, Concepts/The 1990s |Developers=Companies/inngamezstudio |Genres=Genres/Adventure |Platforms=Platforms/PC |Publishers=Companies/Catoptric Games }}

revision.comment: Restore image alignment, sizes and captions from legacy source (3030-41102)
revision.text:
{{Game | Name=Arcade Classics | Guid=3030-41102 | Deck=A collection of three classic Atari arcade games on one Sega cartridge. | ReleaseDate=1996-07-30 | Characters= | Concepts=Concepts/Centipede,Concepts/Flea,Concepts/Scorpions,Concepts/Spiders,Concepts/Tennis | Developers=Companies/Al_Baker_And_Associates | Franchises= | Genres=Genres/Compilation | Locations= | Objects=Objects/Missile,Objects/Mushroom | Platforms=Platforms/Game_Gear,Platforms/Genesis | Publishers=Companies/Sega | Games=Games/Arcade_Classics_1996,Games/Atari_Classics_Evolved | Themes= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/16/164924/2410074-generic.png }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2410074-generic.png","path":"16\/164924\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_avatar,scale_avatar,square_small,scale_small,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,square_mini,square_tiny,square_medium,scale_super,scale_tiny,screen_kubrick","caption":""},"background":[]}' />
{{GBFigure|src=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/16/164924/3095879-2298844558-gfs_3.jpg|full=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/16/164924/3095879-2298844558-gfs_3.jpg|align=right|width=320|caption=Selecting a game (Genesis version)}} The cartridge included:
* [[Games/Centipede|Centipede]] * Ultrapong * [[Games/Missile_Command|Missile Command]] Ultrapong was just titled "[[Games/Pong|Pong]]" on the cover, and was based on the dedicated console release "Ultra Pong Doubles".
Each game included a classic version (which weren't arcade perfect by any stretch of the imagination) and a "Genesis/Sega" version, which slightly enhanced the graphics and/or sound.
